text.title: event-sourcing-implementation Failure Modes
text.summary: Failure modes observed when applying the event-sourcing-implementation methodology. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Two concurrent appends both succeed at version=4; replay diverges.
antipattern.fix: Use UNIQUE (aggregate_id, version) + catch UniqueViolation as ConcurrencyError.

antipattern.symptom: DBA runs UPDATE events SET payload=... to fix a typo; audit trail compromised.
antipattern.fix: REVOKE UPDATE, DELETE ON events FROM PUBLIC; emit a compensating event instead.

antipattern.symptom: Append commits only after projection updates; one slow projection blocks all writes.
antipattern.fix: Decouple: append commits → EventBus async dispatches → projection handlers retry independently.

antipattern.symptom: Aggregate uses datetime.now() inside _apply; replays produce different state.
antipattern.fix: Handlers must be pure functions of the event payload; capture timestamps in the event, not at replay time.
